sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो ।
किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥ २१ ॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho
kim ācāraḥ kathaṁ caitāṁs trīn guṇān ativartate
literal_meaning: By what signs (kaiḥ liṅgaiḥ) is one known to have gone beyond (atītaḥ) these three qualities (trīn guṇān etān), O Lord (prabho)? What is his conduct (kim ācāraḥ), and how (katham) does he pass beyond (ativartate) these three qualities?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks for observable markers of a person who has psychologically outgrown the three innate temperamental forces—tamas (inertia), rajas (activation), and sattva (harmony)—and wants to know the lived behaviors and the process that make such transcendence possible.
psychological_state: A growth-oriented, metacognitive stance—seeking concrete indicators and replicable steps for moving from trait-based living to post-qualitative freedom.
trigger_scenario: A therapy client who has learned about personality types or enneagram asks, 'How will I know I’m not just stuck in my type’s patterns, and what do I actually do to outgrow them?'
anchor_text: Spot, live, and rise above moods that dull, drive, or delight you.
